questions.q: Who was Sahadeva's mother?
questions.explanation: Madri, the princess of Madra and Pandu's second wife, bore the twins Sahadeva and Nakula.

questions.q: Which twin gods fathered Sahadeva and his brother?
questions.explanation: Madri summoned the Ashvins, the twin gods of the dawn, and bore Sahadeva and Nakula.

questions.q: Who was Sahadeva's twin brother?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva was the younger of the twins, born just after his brother Nakula.

questions.q: What was Sahadeva's position among the five Pandava brothers?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva was the youngest of the five Pandavas, the quiet last-born of the brothers.

questions.q: Sahadeva was especially renowned for what?
options: His unmatched strength with the mace
His quiet wisdom and knowledge of the stars
His skill at taming wild elephants
questions.explanation: Sahadeva was the silent seer of the Pandavas, learned in astrology and said to know what was to come.

questions.q: What name did Sahadeva take while hiding in King Virata's service?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva spent the thirteenth year as Tantipala, the keeper of King Virata's cattle.

questions.q: Whom did Sahadeva kill on the eighteenth day of the war, keeping an old vow?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva slew Shakuni, the master of the dice whose game had broken the Pandavas, exactly as he had sworn.

questions.q: Which queen was the shared wife of Sahadeva and his four brothers?
questions.explanation: Draupadi, born of the sacrificial fire, became the common wife of all five Pandavas including Sahadeva.

questions.q: Which king of Hastinapura was Sahadeva's earthly father?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva was a son of King Pandu, born through the Ashvins because Kindama's curse barred Pandu from fathering children himself.

questions.q: Who raised Sahadeva after his mother Madri followed Pandu onto the pyre?
questions.explanation: Madri entrusted her twins to Kunti, who is said to have loved Sahadeva most tenderly of all five boys.

questions.q: Which direction did Sahadeva conquer for Yudhishthira's Rajasuya sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva led the southern campaign, carrying Yudhishthira's authority down to the southern seas.

questions.q: What was the name of Sahadeva's conch?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva blew the Manipushpaka at Kurukshetra, while his twin Nakula sounded the Sughosha.

questions.q: Who was Sahadeva's son by Draupadi, one of the five Upapandavas?
questions.explanation: Shrutasena was Sahadeva's son by Draupadi, youngest of the five Upapandavas.

questions.q: Which son of Shakuni fell to Sahadeva on the war's final day, before Shakuni himself?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva killed Uluka, who had carried Duryodhana's insulting war message, before cutting down his father Shakuni.

questions.q: According to Yudhishthira, what flaw brought Sahadeva down on the final journey?
options: His attachment to cattle and wealth
His silence when Draupadi was wronged
His pride in believing none equalled him in wisdom
His vow of vengeance against Shakuni
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira said the wise Sahadeva fell because he secretly believed no one was his equal in wisdom.

questions.q: Among the five brothers, when did Sahadeva fall on the final journey north?
options: First of the brothers, just after Draupadi
After his twin Nakula had fallen
He alone walked on with Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: After Draupadi fell, Sahadeva was the first of the brothers to drop on the Mahaprasthana, followed by Nakula.

questions.q: At the Yaksha's poisoned lake, Sahadeva was sent to fetch water after which brother?
questions.explanation: When Nakula did not return, Yudhishthira sent Sahadeva next, and he too drank and fell beside his twin.

questions.q: At the Rajasuya sacrifice, what honor was carried out by Sahadeva?
options: Reciting the Vedic hymns of consecration
Distributing gold to the assembled kings
Offering the arghya of first worship to Krishna
questions.explanation: On Bhishma's counsel the first worship went to Krishna, and it was Sahadeva who presented the arghya.

questions.q: Which princess of Madra became Sahadeva's other wife?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva married Vijaya, a princess of his mother's homeland of Madra.

questions.q: What was said of Sahadeva's knowledge of the future?
options: He could foresee only deaths, never births
He knew what was coming yet was bound never to volunteer it
He saw the future only in dreams he forgot at dawn
He gained foresight only after the war was over
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the astrologer Sahadeva knew the shape of things to come but could never speak of it unasked.

questions.q: What duty did Sahadeva perform in disguise at Virata's court?
options: He kept and counted the king's cattle
He cooked in the royal kitchens
He taught dance to the princess
questions.explanation: As Tantipala, Sahadeva tended King Virata's vast herds, claiming to know everything about the ways of cattle.

questions.q: In the dice game, Yudhishthira staked Sahadeva immediately after which brother?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira staked Nakula first and Sahadeva second, before losing Arjuna, Bhima, and finally himself.

questions.q: During Sahadeva's southern campaign, which ruler of Lanka sent tribute and accepted his suzerainty?
questions.explanation: In the Sabha Parva, Sahadeva sent an envoy to Vibhishana of Lanka, who honored Yudhishthira with jewels and tribute.

questions.q: Which god barred Sahadeva's army at Mahishmati during the southern campaign?
questions.explanation: Agni protected King Nila's city of Mahishmati and burned Sahadeva's forces until the prince prayed to him and the fire god relented.

questions.q: How did Sahadeva's son Shrutasena die?
options: By Karna's hand on the seventeenth day
Slain in his sleep during Ashwatthama's night raid
In the burning of the house of lac
questions.explanation: Shrutasena perished with Draupadi's other four sons when Ashwatthama butchered the sleeping Pandava camp after the war ended.

questions.q: In the Svargarohana Parva, into which gods did Sahadeva and his twin merge after death?
questions.explanation: In the epic's final book each Pandava returned to the god who fathered him, and the twins were seen united with the Ashvins.

questions.q: In a famous later retelling, who came to Sahadeva, the astrologer of the Pandavas, to ask the auspicious hour for the war rites?
questions.explanation: The tale that Duryodhana consulted Sahadeva, who answered honestly despite being his enemy, comes from later regional retellings rather than Vyasa's Sanskrit text.

questions.q: Who was Sahadeva's son by his Madra wife Vijaya?
questions.explanation: Vijaya of Madra bore Sahadeva a son named Suhotra.

questions.q: When Shishupala protested Krishna's first worship at the Rajasuya, what did Sahadeva do?
options: He drew his sword and stood before Krishna
He recited Krishna's hundred names in answer
He raised his foot, daring any objector to bear it on his head
He cast Shishupala's seat out of the hall
questions.explanation: In the Sabha Parva, Sahadeva lifted his foot and declared he would set it on the head of anyone who opposed Krishna's worship, and no king dared answer.

questions.q: Before Krishna's peace embassy, what stand did Sahadeva take in the Pandava council?
options: He begged that war be avoided at any cost
He asked to meet Shakuni alone in single combat
He advised the brothers to wait one more year
He declared war must come even if the others made peace, to avenge Draupadi
questions.explanation: In the Udyoga Parva the quiet Sahadeva broke his silence to demand war for Draupadi's humiliation even if his brothers chose peace, and Draupadi blessed him for it.
